content_en:
There is a form of imprisonment that has nothing to do with physical confinement — the imprisonment of a heart that has made itself dependent on something other than Allah for its sense of worth, security, and wellbeing. Yasmin Mogahed examines this condition with the precision of someone who has observed it carefully and the compassion of someone who has experienced it. The imprisoned heart is not rare or extreme; it is the condition of the human heart in its default setting, and reclaiming it is the fundamental task of Islamic spiritual life.

The heart becomes imprisoned through a process that Mogahed traces carefully. It begins with a natural human need — for love, security, validation, purpose, beauty. These needs are not shameful; they are part of how Allah created human beings, and they point toward the One who is their ultimate source and satisfaction. But the heart, living in a world full of created things that seem to offer these satisfactions, often reaches toward those things rather than toward Allah — toward people for the love and validation that only Allah can fully provide, toward wealth for the security that only Allah can guarantee, toward beauty for the transcendence that only the beauty of Allah truly offers.

The mechanism of imprisonment is attachment in the specific sense that Mogahed defines: placing something at the center of one's psychological world in such a way that its presence or absence determines one's inner state. The person whose sense of worth depends on another's approval is imprisoned to that person's judgment. The person whose happiness depends on a specific outcome is imprisoned to that outcome. The person who has built their identity around a particular role — parent, spouse, professional — is imprisoned to the continuity of that role. When the dependency object is present or performs as desired, there is a semblance of peace; when it is absent or fails, there is devastation.

Mogahed draws on the Islamic concept of ghayr Allah — other than Allah — to describe the wide category of things, people, and circumstances that the heart can be imprisoned to. The problem is not the things themselves — human love, material sufficiency, social belonging are genuine goods. The problem is the total dependence of the heart's peace on their presence. This dependence is problematic on two levels: spiritually, because it represents the displacement of Allah from His rightful position as the heart's central love and ultimate reliance; practically, because the things we depend on are always precarious, impermanent, and ultimately unable to deliver the unconditional security we seek from them.

The experience of this imprisonment is familiar: the anxiety that accompanies any threat to a cherished attachment; the obsessive quality of thought about the attachment object; the disproportionate pain when the attachment is threatened or lost; the gradual subordination of other goods — including religious practice, other relationships, and one's own wellbeing — to the maintenance of the attachment. These are not signs of deep love but of dependency, and the suffering they produce is the heart's signal that it has been placed in a prison it was not designed for.

Mogahed presents the Islamic diagnosis not to condemn the reader but to offer a path. The imprisoned heart can be freed — not by ceasing to love or care, but by redirecting the ultimate weight of its reliance toward the only One whose support is truly unconditional. This redirection is not a one-time act but a continuous practice of remembrance, prayer, and conscious reorientation — the daily spiritual work that gradually loosens the heart's dependency on created things and returns it to the freedom of genuine tawakkul.
content_id:
Ada bentuk pemenjaraan yang tidak ada hubungannya dengan penahanan fisik — pemenjaraan hati yang telah membuat dirinya bergantung pada sesuatu selain Allah untuk rasa harga diri, keamanan, dan kesejahteraannya. Yasmin Mogahed mengkaji kondisi ini dengan ketepatan seseorang yang telah mengamatinya dengan cermat dan belas kasih seseorang yang telah mengalaminya. Hati yang terbelenggu bukanlah sesuatu yang langka atau ekstrem; itu adalah kondisi hati manusia dalam pengaturan bawaannya, dan merebut kembalinya adalah tugas fundamental kehidupan spiritual Islam.

Hati menjadi terbelenggu melalui proses yang Mogahed telusuri dengan cermat. Dimulai dengan kebutuhan manusiawi yang alami — untuk cinta, keamanan, validasi, tujuan, keindahan. Kebutuhan-kebutuhan ini tidak memalukan; mereka adalah bagian dari bagaimana Allah menciptakan manusia, dan mereka menunjuk kepada Dia yang merupakan sumber dan kepuasan tertinggi mereka. Tetapi hati, hidup di dunia yang penuh dengan hal-hal yang diciptakan yang tampaknya menawarkan kepuasan-kepuasan ini, sering menjangkau hal-hal tersebut daripada kepada Allah — kepada orang-orang untuk cinta dan validasi yang hanya Allah yang dapat sepenuhnya memberikannya, kepada kekayaan untuk keamanan yang hanya Allah yang dapat menjaminnya, kepada keindahan untuk transendensi yang hanya keindahan Allah yang benar-benar menawarkannya.

Mekanisme pemenjaraan adalah keterikatan dalam arti spesifik yang Mogahed definisikan: menempatkan sesuatu di pusat dunia psikologis seseorang sedemikian rupa sehingga kehadiran atau ketidakhadirannya menentukan keadaan batin seseorang. Orang yang rasa harga dirinya bergantung pada persetujuan orang lain terbelenggu pada penilaian orang itu. Orang yang kebahagiaannya bergantung pada hasil tertentu terbelenggu pada hasil itu. Orang yang telah membangun identitasnya di sekitar peran tertentu — orang tua, pasangan, profesional — terbelenggu pada keberlangsungan peran itu. Ketika objek ketergantungan hadir atau berfungsi sebagaimana diinginkan, ada gambaran kedamaian; ketika tidak ada atau gagal, ada kehancuran.

Mogahed mengambil dari konsep Islam ghayr Allah — selain Allah — untuk menggambarkan kategori luas dari hal-hal, orang-orang, dan keadaan-keadaan yang hati dapat terbelenggu padanya. Masalahnya bukan pada hal-hal itu sendiri — cinta manusiawi, kecukupan materi, kepemilikan sosial adalah kebaikan-kebaikan sejati. Masalahnya adalah ketergantungan total dari kedamaian hati pada kehadiran mereka. Ketergantungan ini bermasalah pada dua tingkat: secara spiritual, karena ia mewakili penggeseran Allah dari posisi yang layak-Nya sebagai cinta utama dan ketergantungan tertinggi hati; secara praktis, karena hal-hal yang kita andalkan selalu tidak aman, tidak kekal, dan pada akhirnya tidak mampu memberikan keamanan tanpa syarat yang kita cari dari mereka.

Pengalaman pemenjaraan ini sudah akrab: kecemasan yang menyertai setiap ancaman terhadap keterikatan yang dihargai; kualitas obsesif dari pikiran tentang objek keterikatan; rasa sakit yang tidak proporsional ketika keterikatan terancam atau hilang; subordinasi bertahap dari kebaikan-kebaikan lain — termasuk amalan agama, hubungan-hubungan lain, dan kesejahteraan seseorang sendiri — terhadap pemeliharaan keterikatan. Ini bukan tanda-tanda cinta yang dalam tetapi ketergantungan, dan penderitaan yang mereka hasilkan adalah sinyal hati bahwa ia telah ditempatkan dalam penjara yang tidak dirancang untuknya.

Mogahed menyajikan diagnosis Islam bukan untuk mengutuk pembaca tetapi untuk menawarkan jalan. Hati yang terbelenggu dapat dibebaskan — bukan dengan berhenti mencintai atau peduli, tetapi dengan mengarahkan ulang bobot tertinggi dari ketergantungannya menuju satu-satunya Dia yang dukungannya benar-benar tanpa syarat. Pengarahan ulang ini bukan tindakan satu kali tetapi praktik dzikir, shalat, dan reorientasi sadar yang berkelanjutan — pekerjaan spiritual harian yang secara bertahap melonggarkan ketergantungan hati pada hal-hal yang diciptakan dan mengembalikannya ke kebebasan tawakkal yang sejati.
content_ar: There is a form of imprisonment that has nothing to do with physical confinement — the imprisonment of a القلب that has made itself dependent on something other than الله for its sense of worth, security, and wellbeing. Yasmin Mogahed examines this condition with the precision of someone who has observed it carefully and the الرحمة of someone who has experienced it. The imprisoned القلب is not rare or extreme; it is the condition of the human القلب in its default setting, and reclaiming it is the الأساسي task of Islamic spiritual الحياة. The القلب becomes imprisoned through a process that Mogahed traces carefully. It begins with a natural human need — for المحبة, security, validation, purpose, beauty. These needs are not shameful; they are part of how الله created human beings, and they point toward the One who is their ultimate source and satisfaction. But the القلب, living in a الدنيا full of created things that seem to offer these satisfactions, often reaches toward those things rather than toward الله — toward people for the المحبة and validation that only الله can fully provide, toward الثروة for the security that only الله can guarantee, toward beauty for the transcendence that only the beauty of الله truly offers. The mechanism of imprisonment is attachment in the specific sense that Mogahed defines: placing something at the center of one's psychological الدنيا in such a الطريق that its presence or absence determines one's inner state. The person whose sense of worth depends on another's approval is imprisoned to that person's judgment. The person whose happiness depends on a specific outcome is imprisoned to that outcome. The person who has built their identity around a particular role — parent, spouse, professional — is imprisoned to the continuity of that role. عندما the dependency object is present or performs as desired, there is a semblance of peace; عندما it is absent or fails, there is devastation. Mogahed draws on the Islamic concept of ghayr الله — other than الله — to describe the wide category of things, people, and circumstances that the القلب can be imprisoned to. The problem is not the things themselves — human المحبة, material sufficiency, social belonging are genuine goods. The problem is the total dependence of the القلب's peace on their presence. This dependence is problematic on two levels: spiritually, لأن it represents the displacement of الله from His rightful position as the القلب's المحوري المحبة and ultimate reliance; practically, لأن the things we depend on are always precarious, impermanent, and ultimately unable to deliver the unconditional security we seek from them. The experience of this imprisonment is familiar: the القلق that accompanies any threat to a cherished attachment; the obsessive quality of thought about the attachment object; the disproportionate الألم عندما the attachment is threatened or lost; the gradual subordination of other goods — بما في ذلك religious
